concept_decisions.evidence_inspected: Manifest hashes and row counts; concept table; titles and complete available abstracts for all assignment-input records, with focused review of all comparator-based time-series papers.
concept_decisions.rationale: Comparator-based segmented designs remain a coherent concept distinct from simple ITS. Comparative ITS, controlled ITS, and nonequivalent-control-outcome variants belong here; several such records were misrouted under simple ITS and were reassigned in, while two simple ITS records were reassigned out.

concept_decisions.evidence_inspected: Manifest hashes and row counts; concept table; assigned record title and complete abstract.
concept_decisions.rationale: The assigned paper explicitly uses staggered DiD with heterogeneity-robust identification, which should remain separate from legacy DiD/event-study designs.

concept_decisions.evidence_inspected: Manifest hashes and row counts; concept table; titles and complete available abstracts for all instrumental-variable assignments.
concept_decisions.rationale: The assigned records consistently center instrumental-variable identification, spanning provider-preference instruments, policy/natural-experiment instruments, randomized-nonadherence IV analyses, and IV methods papers. No additional split was needed within this batch.

concept_decisions.retained_canonical_name: Interrupted time series and segmented regression
concept_decisions.labels_compared: interrupted_time_series vs controlled_interrupted_time_series vs synthetic_control_designs vs target_trial_emulation vs systematic_review_meta_analysis vs causal_methods_comparison_guidance
concept_decisions.evidence_inspected: Manifest hashes and row counts; concept table; titles and complete available abstracts for all assignment-input records, with focused review of all time-series assignments containing comparator, target-trial, synthetic-control, review, or correction language.
concept_decisions.rationale: Simple ITS and segmented regression remain a coherent concept after removing comparator-based ITS, mixed-design guidance papers, evidence syntheses, target-trial emulations, synthetic-control analyses, one DiD paper, and one correction notice.

concept_decisions.labels_compared: difference_in_differences_event_study vs heterogeneity_robust_staggered_did vs synthetic_control_designs vs causal_methods_comparison_guidance vs time_to_event_cohort_studies
concept_decisions.evidence_inspected: Manifest hashes and row counts; concept table; titles and complete available abstracts for all DiD/event-study assignments, with focused review of mixed-method and false-positive event-study hits.
concept_decisions.rationale: Legacy DiD and event-study designs remain semantically coherent and distinct from heterogeneity-robust staggered DiD. False-positive event-study matches, a correction notice, and cross-design methods guidance were reassigned out.

concept_decisions.retained_canonical_name: Marginal structural models and inverse-probability weighting
concept_decisions.evidence_inspected: Manifest hashes and row counts; concept table; assigned record title and complete abstract.
concept_decisions.rationale: The assigned paper is explicitly an MSM/IPTW methods paper and should remain separate from IV and other longitudinal causal designs.

concept_decisions.labels_compared: negative_controls vs test_negative_designs vs self_controlled_case_only_designs vs diagnostic_accuracy_studies vs randomized_controlled_trials vs case_reports
concept_decisions.evidence_inspected: Manifest hashes and row counts; concept table; titles and complete available abstracts for all negative-control assignments, with focused review of test-negative, self-controlled, diagnostic, trial-arm, and assay-interference uses of 'negative control'.
concept_decisions.rationale: After splitting out test-negative designs and self-controlled case-only variants, and removing non-causal uses of 'negative control', the retained set coheres around negative-control outcome/exposure/population logic for bias detection or adjustment.

concept_decisions.retained_canonical_name: Not retained; redistributed to concrete concepts
concept_decisions.labels_compared: quasi_experimental_policy_evaluation vs interrupted_time_series vs negative_controls vs test_negative_designs vs target_trial_emulation vs stepped_wedge_cluster_randomized_trials vs synthetic_control_designs vs causal_methods_comparison_guidance vs systematic_review_meta_analysis vs transmission_dynamic_modeling vs observational_pathogen_association_regression
concept_decisions.evidence_inspected: Manifest hashes and row counts; concept table; titles and complete available abstracts for all records provisionally assigned to the quasi_experimental_policy_evaluation fallback bucket.
concept_decisions.rationale: This label is an explicit fallback bucket mixing concrete designs that are semantically distinct. No vague residual 'other quasi-experimental' concept should remain final for this batch.

reassignments.rationale: Explicit intervention and control wards make this a controlled ITS rather than simple ITS.
The abstract explicitly states controlled interrupted time-series analysis with flooded versus nonflooded areas.
Monthly carbapenem use is analyzed with a controlled ITS using vancomycin as the control series.
The study compares implementing hospitals with nonimplementing control hospitals in a multicenter controlled ITS.
Hospital-onset bacteremia is evaluated with community-acquired bacteremia as a nonequivalent dependent control.
The abstract explicitly identifies a controlled interrupted time-series design with pilot and comparison facilities.
The abstract explicitly identifies a controlled interrupted time-series analysis using rotavirus-negative cases as the control series.
Hospital-acquired resistance outcomes are analyzed in a controlled ITS with community-acquired outcomes as controls.
The abstract explicitly states a controlled interrupted time series with outbreak units versus control units.
Although the title is generic ITS, the abstract adds analyses with control series, making the final design controlled ITS.
Hospitals are explicitly classified into intervention and control groups and compared through ITS.
The study explicitly uses a two-stage controlled interrupted time-series design.
The abstract explicitly identifies a multinational controlled interrupted time-series study.
Historical ITS is paired with a contemporaneous comparison group of noneligible children.
The abstract explicitly states a balanced interrupted time series with control.

reassignments.to_canonical_name: Interrupted time series and segmented regression
reassignments.rationale: The primary design is a simple interrupted-time-series analysis of outpatient visits before and after policy change.

reassignments.to_canonical_name: Interrupted time series and segmented regression
reassignments.rationale: The abstract explicitly identifies an uncontrolled interrupted time series.

reassignments.to_canonical_name: Interrupted time series and segmented regression
reassignments.rationale: Facility-level impact is estimated by time-series analysis without a comparator series.

reassignments.to_canonical_name: Interrupted time series and segmented regression
reassignments.rationale: The primary design is a multistage interrupted-time-series analysis.

reassignments.to_canonical_name: Interrupted time series and segmented regression
reassignments.rationale: The primary design is simple ITS on weekly AMR prevalence; controlled ITS appears only as secondary subgroup comparison.

reassignments.to_canonical_name: Interrupted time series and segmented regression
reassignments.rationale: The primary design is a single-center interrupted-time-series study.
The primary comparative identification strategy is explicitly difference-in-differences.
The core bias-checking device is an RSV negative-control outcome for waning influenza vaccine effectiveness.
The abstract explicitly centers the test-negative control design and alternative control-group choices.
The record is explicitly a case test-negative control design study.
The title and abstract describe a case-test negative-control vaccine-effectiveness study.
The study compares influenza-positive and influenza-negative pneumonia patients in a test-negative case-control framework.
The study explicitly uses rotavirus-test-negative controls to estimate vaccine effectiveness.
The title and abstract explicitly identify a test-negative control study.
The paper develops a real-time extension of the test-negative design.
The paper is specifically about bias detection within test-negative case-control studies.
The record belongs to hospital-based influenza vaccine-effectiveness estimation using a test-negative framework.
The title explicitly identifies a test-negative control study.
The cohort is explicitly constructed with a test-negative control group.
The title explicitly identifies a test-negative control study.
The study estimates vaccine effectiveness in a hospital-based test-negative design.
The title explicitly identifies a test-negative design analysis.
The primary comparator is a test-negative control cohort.
The paper is explicitly about the validity of the test-negative design.

reassignments.to_canonical_name: Self-controlled risk-interval and self-matched case-only designs
reassignments.rationale: The paper introduces a self-matched case-only negative-control design.

reassignments.to_canonical_name: Self-controlled risk-interval and self-matched case-only designs
reassignments.rationale: The primary design is self-controlled risk-interval.

reassignments.to_canonical_name: Self-controlled risk-interval and self-matched case-only designs
reassignments.rationale: The primary design is self-controlled risk-interval with negative-control outcomes used to probe time-varying confounding.
This is an evidence synthesis rather than an original ITS study.
This is a systematic review and network meta-analysis rather than an original ITS study.
This is a systematic review and network meta-analysis rather than an original ITS study.
This is a systematic review and meta-analysis of test-negative studies rather than a primary test-negative study.
This is a systematic review rather than an original ITS study.
This is a systematic review and reanalysis of quasi-experimental studies rather than a single ITS study.
This is a systematic review and meta-analysis rather than an original ITS study.
This is a systematic review and evidence map rather than an original ITS study.
This is a systematic review and quantitative synthesis rather than a primary quasi-experimental design study.
This is a systematic review update rather than an original ITS study.
The abstract explicitly states matched retrospective target-trial cohort studies.
The primary design is explicit target-trial emulation; supporting ITS is secondary.
The primary design is explicit target-trial emulation.
The paper is specifically about stepped-wedge cluster-randomized trial properties in outbreaks.
The primary comparative identification strategy uses synthetic control units within a multiple-ITS framework.
The core counterfactual is a synthetic control constructed from other causes of death.
The paper is a cross-design overview of quasi-experimental designs rather than a single retained empirical design.
The paper compares randomized and observational effect estimation protocols rather than instantiating one retained design.
The paper is a cross-design guide covering pre/post, ITS, and DiD rather than an ITS application.
The paper explicitly compares DiD and synthetic control methods with case studies.
The paper is a triangulation/comparison methods article spanning IV, confounder adjustment, and DiD.
The primary design is a longitudinal time-to-event cohort analysis, not an econometric event-study design.
The primary design is a case-control time-to-event survival study, not a DiD/event-study design.
The primary design is observational mixed-effects regression of exposure-marker associations, not quasi-experimental policy evaluation.
The paper introduces and applies a mathematical epidemicity index in dynamic transmission models.
The primary design is diagnostic accuracy and monitoring using negative-control specimens, not causal negative-control inference.
The primary design is a randomized treatment comparison with a negative-control arm, not a negative-control causal-inference design.
This is a case report about assay interference rather than a negative-control causal design.
This publication is a correction notice, not an original DiD study.
This publication is a correction notice, not an original ITS study.

new_concepts.rationale: Needed to separate classic test-negative case-control and test-negative control-cohort designs from broader negative-control outcome/exposure studies.

new_concepts.canonical_name: Self-controlled risk-interval and self-matched case-only designs
new_concepts.rationale: Needed to preserve within-person/case-only designs that rely on self-controlled or self-matched identification rather than external controls.
Needed for evidence syntheses misrouted into empirical design concepts.
Needed for records explicitly framed as target-trial emulations.
Needed to preserve stepped-wedge trials as a distinct trial design rather than leaving them in a fallback quasi-experimental bucket.
Needed to preserve synthetic-control identification as distinct from plain ITS and legacy DiD.
Needed for cross-design guidance, harmonization, and triangulation papers that do not instantiate one retained empirical design.
Needed for false-positive event-study hits that are actually survival/time-to-event observational studies.
Needed for non-quasi-experimental regression studies of environmental markers and pathogen detection.
Needed for epidemic-dynamics modeling papers that are not quasi-experimental evaluations.
Needed for diagnostic studies using negative-control specimens in a laboratory/measurement sense rather than a causal-inference sense.
Needed for randomized intervention comparisons misrouted because a control arm was labeled 'negative control'.
Needed for single-patient or single-case publications misrouted by negative-control wording.
Needed so corrigenda/errata do not remain assigned as original study designs.

concerns: ordered_key_sha256 was computed as SHA256 of candidate_key values in assignment-input row order joined by a single newline; the manifest does not specify the serialization convention.

Several records combine multiple methods (for example, synthetic control plus DiD/event study, or target-trial emulation plus supporting ITS); final assignments follow the primary identification strategy emphasized in the title and abstract.
